translations.literal: He killed himself in the Proclamation Chamber; his body died and was left unburied.
translations.idiomatic: He took his own life in the Proclamation Chamber. His corpse was left unburied.

translations.literal: His head was hung from the rear bar of a chariot, dragged along by four horses.
translations.idiomatic: His severed head was hung from the back of a chariot and dragged through the streets by four horses.

sentences.zh: 寡人念其如此，腸如涫湯。

translations.literal: When I think of how it ended like this, my innards churn like boiling water.
translations.idiomatic: When I contemplate such an end, my insides churn as though filled with boiling water.

sentences.zh: 是人皆富有天下而貴至天子，然而大傲。

translations.literal: These men all possessed the wealth of the realm and attained the nobility of the Son of Heaven, yet they were supremely arrogant.
translations.idiomatic: These men all commanded the wealth of the realm and held the supreme rank of the Son of Heaven, yet they were supremely arrogant.

sentences.zh: 欲無猒時，舉事而喜高，貪很而驕。

translations.literal: Their desires knew no satisfaction; in everything they undertook they sought grandeur, and they were greedy, cruel, and proud.
translations.idiomatic: Their appetites knew no limit. In all their undertakings they craved grandeur, and they were greedy, ruthless, and proud.

sentences.zh: 不用忠信，聽其諛臣，而為天下笑。

translations.literal: They did not employ the loyal and trustworthy, listened to their flattering ministers, and became a laughingstock for the entire world.
translations.idiomatic: They spurned the loyal and trustworthy, heeded their flattering ministers, and became a laughingstock for all under Heaven.

sentences.zh: 今寡人之邦，居諸侯之閒，曾不如秋毫。

translations.literal: Now my state, situated among the feudal lords, is not even the weight of an autumn hair.
translations.idiomatic: My own state, placed as it is among the feudal lords, does not amount to even the tip of an autumn hair in comparison.

translations.literal: If I act improperly, where would I even flee to!'
translations.idiomatic: If I act improperly, where in the world would I escape to!'

translations.literal: Wei Ping replied: 'That is not so.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ping responded: 'That is not how matters stand.

sentences.zh: 河雖神賢，不如崑崙之山；

translations.literal: Although the Yellow River is divine and worthy, it does not compare to Mount Kunlun;
translations.idiomatic: Divine and worthy as the Yellow River may be, it does not compare to Mount Kunlun;

sentences.zh: 江之源理，不如四海，而人尚奪取其寶，諸侯爭之，兵革為起。

translations.literal: the source and course of the Yangtze do not compare to the four seas; yet people still seize and take their treasures, feudal lords contend for them, and wars arise as a result.
translations.idiomatic: and the source of the Yangtze does not compare to the four seas. Yet people still seize the treasures from these waters, feudal lords contend for them, and wars erupt as a result.

sentences.zh: 小國見亡，大國危殆，殺人父兄，虜人妻子，殘國滅廟，以爭此寶。

translations.literal: Small states are destroyed, great states are imperiled; they kill men's fathers and brothers, capture their wives and children, ruin states and destroy ancestral temples — all to contend for these treasures.
translations.idiomatic: Small states are annihilated, great states thrown into peril. Fathers and brothers are killed, wives and children taken captive, states laid waste and ancestral temples razed to the ground — all in the struggle for these treasures.

translations.literal: Warfare, attack, division, and contention — this is tyranny and force.
translations.idiomatic: Warfare, conquest, division, and strife — this is what tyranny and force truly look like.

sentences.zh: 故云取之以暴彊而治以文理，無逆四時，必親賢士；

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: acquire by force and tyranny, but govern with culture and order; do not go against the four seasons, and always draw near to worthy men;
translations.idiomatic: Thus it is said: seize power through force, but govern through culture and principle. Do not defy the rhythm of the four seasons, and always keep worthy men close;

translations.literal: harmonize with the transformations of yin and yang, and let the ghosts and spirits serve as your envoys;
translations.idiomatic: move in harmony with the transformations of yin and yang, and let the spirits serve as your agents;

translations.literal: communicate with heaven and earth, and make them your friends.
translations.idiomatic: commune with heaven and earth, and make them your allies.

translations.literal: The feudal lords will submit as guests, and the multitudes will rejoice abundantly.
translations.idiomatic: The feudal lords will pledge their submission, and the people will rejoice.

translations.literal: The state and the households will be at peace, and the age will be renewed.
translations.idiomatic: The state will be at peace, and the world will begin anew.

translations.literal: Tang and Wu practiced this, and thereby won the title of Son of Heaven;
translations.idiomatic: Tang and Wu followed this path and thereby won the throne of the Son of Heaven;

translations.literal: the Spring and Autumn Annals recorded it as a governing principle.
translations.idiomatic: the Spring and Autumn Annals set it down as a guiding principle for all ages.

sentences.zh: 王不自稱湯武，而自比桀紂。

translations.literal: Yet the king does not compare himself to Tang and Wu, but likens himself to Jie and Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Your Majesty does not liken yourself to Tang and Wu, but instead compares yourself to Jie and Zhou.

sentences.zh: 桀紂為暴彊也，固以為常。

translations.literal: The tyranny and violence of Jie and Zhou were taken as normal by them.
translations.idiomatic: The tyranny and brutality of Jie and Zhou were something they regarded as perfectly normal.

translations.literal: Jie built a tile palace, and Zhou built the Ivory Gallery.
translations.idiomatic: Jie built a palace of tiles, and Zhou constructed the Ivory Gallery.

translations.literal: They levied silk to adorn these, devoting themselves to squandering the people's resources.
translations.idiomatic: They levied silk to adorn these extravagances, intent on squandering the people's resources.

translations.literal: Taxes and levies were without measure, killings and slaughters without restraint.
translations.idiomatic: Taxes were levied without limit, and killing was carried out without restraint.

translations.literal: They killed people and livestock, and used leather to make bags.
translations.idiomatic: They slaughtered people and livestock alike, using leather to fashion bags.

sentences.zh: 囊盛其血，與人縣而射之，與天帝爭彊。

translations.literal: They filled the bags with blood, hung them up, and had people shoot at them — contending in force with the Heavenly Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: They filled the bags with blood, hung them aloft, and had men shoot arrows at them — vying in arrogance with the Heavenly Emperor himself.

translations.literal: They defied and disrupted the four seasons, tasting food before the hundred spirits.
translations.idiomatic: They defied the natural order of the four seasons and tasted the offerings before the hundred spirits.

translations.literal: Those who remonstrated were promptly killed; flatterers stayed at their side.
translations.idiomatic: Anyone who dared remonstrate was put to death, while flatterers remained ever at their side.

translations.literal: Sages went into hiding, and the common people dared not move.
translations.idiomatic: Sages went into hiding, and the common people were paralyzed with fear.

translations.literal: Heaven repeatedly sent drought, and the state was plagued by many monstrous portents.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven visited drought upon the land again and again, and the state was beset with monstrous portents.

translations.literal: Crop-eating insects were born every year, and the five grains did not ripen.
translations.idiomatic: Crop-devouring insects appeared year after year, and the five grains failed to ripen.

translations.literal: The people could not rest easy in their dwellings, and the ghosts and spirits refused their offerings.
translations.idiomatic: The people found no peace in their homes, and the spirits refused their sacrificial offerings.

translations.literal: Whirlwinds arose daily, and broad daylight turned to darkness.
translations.idiomatic: Whirlwinds howled daily, and midday turned dark as night.

translations.literal: The sun and moon were both eclipsed, extinguished and without light.
translations.idiomatic: Both the sun and the moon were eclipsed, extinguished and robbed of all their light.

translations.literal: The ranked stars rushed about in confusion, all severed from their guiding order.
translations.idiomatic: The stars fell from their courses and flew about in disorder, all guiding principles shattered.

translations.literal: Viewed from this perspective, how could such a state last long!
translations.idiomatic: Seen in this light, how could such a state possibly endure!

translations.literal: Even without Tang and Wu, the time had come for it to perish.
translations.idiomatic: Even without the advent of Tang and Wu, the time had come for it to perish.

sentences.zh: 故湯伐桀，武王剋紂，其時使然。

translations.literal: Therefore when Tang attacked Jie and King Wu vanquished Zhou, it was the appointed time that brought it about.
translations.idiomatic: And so when Tang overthrew Jie and King Wu vanquished Zhou of Shang, the appointed time had decreed it.

translations.literal: They became Sons of Heaven, and their descendants carried on through the ages;
translations.idiomatic: They became Sons of Heaven, and their descendants continued the line through the generations;

sentences.zh: 終身無咎，後世稱之，至今不已。

translations.literal: Throughout their lives they incurred no blame; later generations praised them, and to this day the praise has not ceased.
translations.idiomatic: Throughout their lives they suffered no reproach. Posterity has praised them ever since, and the acclaim continues to this day.

sentences.zh: 是皆當時而行，見事而彊，乃能成其帝王。

translations.literal: They all acted when the time was right, saw their opportunity and exerted force, and thereby achieved their imperial kingship.
translations.idiomatic: They all acted at the right moment, seized the opportunity with decisive force, and thereby achieved the rank of emperor and king.

sentences.zh: 今龜，大寶也，為聖人使，傳之賢（士）［王］。

translations.literal: Now this tortoise is a great treasure; it serves as the envoy of sages and is transmitted to worthy kings.
translations.idiomatic: This tortoise is a supreme treasure. It serves as an emissary of the sages and is bestowed upon worthy kings.

translations.literal: It requires no hands or feet; thunder and lightning escort it;
translations.idiomatic: It needs no hands or feet — thunder and lightning escort it;

translations.literal: wind and rain send it forth, and flowing water carries it along.
translations.idiomatic: wind and rain convey it, and flowing water bears it on its way.

translations.literal: Only a lord or king who possesses virtue may be worthy of receiving it.
translations.idiomatic: Only a lord or king of true virtue may be deemed worthy to receive it.

sentences.zh: 今王有德而當此寶，恐不敢受；

translations.literal: Now the king possesses virtue and is worthy of this treasure, yet fears and dares not accept it;
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty possesses the virtue to be worthy of this treasure, yet you hesitate and dare not accept;

translations.literal: If the king releases it, Song will surely suffer blame.
translations.idiomatic: if you release it, the state of Song will surely incur blame.

translations.literal: Though you may later regret it, it will be too late.'
translations.idiomatic: Even should you come to regret it afterward, it will be far too late.'

translations.literal: King Yuan was greatly pleased and delighted.
translations.idiomatic: King Yuan was overjoyed.

sentences.zh: 於是元王向日而謝，再拜而受。

translations.literal: Thereupon King Yuan faced the sun and gave thanks, bowed twice, and accepted the tortoise.
translations.idiomatic: He turned to face the sun, offered thanks, bowed twice, and accepted the tortoise.

translations.literal: He chose an auspicious day for fasting and purification; a jia or yi day was most favorable.
translations.idiomatic: He chose an auspicious day for fasting and purification — a jia or yi day being the most propitious.

translations.literal: He then sacrificed a white pheasant and a black sheep;
translations.idiomatic: He sacrificed a white pheasant and a black sheep;

translations.literal: he poured their blood over the tortoise at the center of the altar.
translations.idiomatic: their blood was poured over the tortoise at the center of the altar.

translations.literal: He peeled it with a knife; its body remained whole and uninjured.
translations.idiomatic: With a knife, the shell was removed, yet the body remained whole and unharmed.

translations.literal: He offered it dried meat and wine as ritual gifts, and laid out its belly and entrails.
translations.idiomatic: Dried meat and wine were offered in ritual, and the entrails were laid out.

translations.literal: He divined it with a thorn branch, making sure to control the incision.
translations.idiomatic: A thorned branch was used for the divination, the incision carefully controlled.

translations.literal: The lines of the cracks extended clearly, and the patterns interlocked and responded to each other.
translations.idiomatic: The lines of the cracks spread clearly, and the patterns interlocked and answered one another.

translations.literal: He had skilled diviners interpret it; every pronouncement proved correct.
translations.idiomatic: Skilled diviners were called to interpret the results, and every pronouncement proved correct.

translations.literal: The state's fortune and this precious treasure became known to the neighboring lands.
translations.idiomatic: The state's good fortune and this precious treasure became renowned throughout the neighboring lands.

translations.literal: Oxen were slaughtered and their hides taken, and covered with tong wood from Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Oxen were slaughtered for their hides, which were stretched over tong wood from Zheng to make drums and shields.

translations.literal: All the plants and trees were divided and transformed into armor and weapons.
translations.idiomatic: Every kind of wood and plant was allocated and fashioned into armor and weapons.

translations.literal: In winning battles and seizing conquests, none surpassed King Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: In winning battles and conquering territories, no one could rival King Yuan.

sentences.zh: 元王之時，衛平相宋，宋國最彊，龜之力也。

translations.literal: In the time of King Yuan, Wei Ping served as chancellor of Song, and the state of Song was the most powerful — this was the power of the tortoise.
translations.idiomatic: In the time of King Yuan, Wei Ping served as chancellor of Song, and Song became the most powerful state of all. This was the power of the tortoise.

sentences.zh: 故云神至能見夢於元王，而不能自出漁者之籠。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: the divine tortoise was able to appear in a dream to King Yuan, yet it could not free itself from the fisherman's cage.
translations.idiomatic: Thus it is said: the divine tortoise, for all its power, could appear in King Yuan's dream yet could not free itself from a fisherman's cage.

sentences.zh: 身能十言盡當，不能通使於河，還報於江，賢能令人戰勝攻取，不能自解於刀鋒，免剝刺之患。

translations.literal: Its pronouncements were correct in all ten out of ten cases, yet it could not complete its embassy to the Yellow River and return its report to the Yangtze; its worthiness could enable men to win battles and conquer, yet it could not free itself from the blade's edge and escape the peril of being flayed and pierced.
translations.idiomatic: Its every pronouncement was correct, yet it could not complete its mission to the Yellow River and report back to the Yangtze. Its powers could bring men victory in battle and conquest, yet it could not save itself from the blade's edge and escape the peril of being flayed and pierced.

sentences.zh: 聖能先知亟見，而不能令衛平無言。

translations.literal: Its saintly power could foresee events and manifest frequently, yet it could not make Wei Ping hold his tongue.
translations.idiomatic: Its prescience could foresee events and make itself manifest, yet it could not silence Wei Ping.

translations.literal: In pronouncing on affairs it was perfect a hundred times, but when it came to its own person it was helpless;
translations.idiomatic: In pronouncing on the affairs of others it was flawless every time, but when it came to its own fate it was utterly helpless;

translations.literal: When the time is unfavorable, what use is worthiness!
translations.idiomatic: when the moment turns against you, what good is even the greatest wisdom!

translations.literal: The worthy have their constant nature, and gentlemen have their contingent circumstances.
translations.idiomatic: The worthy have their constant nature, and even gentlemen are subject to the vagaries of circumstance.

sentences.zh: 是故明有所不見，聽有所不聞；

translations.literal: Therefore even sight has things it does not see, and even hearing has things it does not hear;
translations.idiomatic: And so even sight has its blind spots, and even hearing has its deaf moments;

sentences.zh: 人雖賢，不能左畫方，右畫圓；

translations.literal: Though a person may be worthy, he cannot draw a square with his left hand and a circle with his right at the same time;
translations.idiomatic: however worthy a person may be, he cannot draw a square with his left hand and a circle with his right simultaneously;

sentences.zh: 日月之明，而時蔽於浮雲。

translations.literal: the sun and moon are brilliant, yet are sometimes obscured by drifting clouds.
translations.idiomatic: the sun and moon shine brilliantly, yet even they are sometimes veiled by drifting clouds.

sentences.zh: 羿名善射，不如雄渠、蜂門；

translations.literal: Yi was famed as a great archer, yet fell short of Xiongqu and Fengmen;
translations.idiomatic: The legendary archer Yi was renowned for his skill, yet even he was surpassed by Xiongqu and Fengmen;

sentences.zh: 禹名為辯智，而不能勝鬼神。

translations.literal: Yu was known for his eloquence and wisdom, yet he could not prevail over ghosts and spirits.
translations.idiomatic: Yu was celebrated for his eloquence and wisdom, yet even he could not prevail over the spirits.

sentences.zh: 地柱折，天故毋椽，又柰何責人於全？

translations.literal: When the pillars of the earth have broken and the sky itself lacks rafters, how can you demand perfection from a person?
translations.idiomatic: When the very pillars of the earth have crumbled and the sky itself has no rafters, how can one demand perfection of any creature?

sentences.zh: 孔子聞之曰：「神龜知吉凶，而骨直空枯。

translations.literal: When Confucius heard of this, he said: 'The divine tortoise can discern fortune and misfortune, yet its bones end up dry and empty.
translations.idiomatic: When Confucius heard of this, he said: 'The divine tortoise can discern fortune and misfortune, yet in the end its bones are left dry and hollow.

sentences.zh: 日為德而君於天下，辱於三足之烏。

translations.literal: The sun is virtuous and rules over all beneath the sky, yet is shamed by the three-legged crow.
translations.idiomatic: The sun reigns in virtue over all under heaven, yet is shamed by the three-legged crow that dwells within it.

sentences.zh: 月為刑而相佐，見食於蝦蟆。

translations.literal: The moon governs punishment and assists, yet is devoured by the toad.
translations.idiomatic: The moon governs punishment and serves as the sun's companion, yet even it is devoured by the toad.

sentences.zh: 猬辱於鵲，騰蛇之神而殆於即且。

translations.literal: The hedgehog is humiliated by the magpie; the soaring serpent, though divine, is endangered by the centipede.
translations.idiomatic: The hedgehog is humbled by the magpie, and the divine soaring serpent meets its peril at the fangs of the centipede.

translations.literal: Bamboo has joints and grain on the outside, but inside it is straight and hollow;
translations.idiomatic: Bamboo has joints and grain on the outside, yet inside it is straight and hollow;

sentences.zh: 松柏為百木長，而守門閭。

translations.literal: The pine and cypress are the chiefs of all trees, yet they stand guard at gates and doorways.
translations.idiomatic: the pine and cypress are the noblest of all trees, yet they are posted as sentinels at gates and doorways.

translations.literal: The cycle of days and celestial stems is not perfect, and so there are gaps and voids.
translations.idiomatic: The cycle of days and celestial stems is imperfect, and so there are gaps and voids.

translations.literal: Gold has its flaws, and white jade has its blemishes.
translations.idiomatic: Even gold has its flaws, and white jade has its blemishes.

translations.literal: In affairs, some things are swift, and some are slow.
translations.idiomatic: In the course of events, some things move swiftly and others slowly.

translations.literal: Things have their constraints, and also their supports.
translations.idiomatic: All things have their constraints, and all have their supports.

translations.literal: Nets have their closely woven parts, and also their loosely woven parts.
translations.idiomatic: A net has its closely woven parts and its loosely woven parts.

translations.literal: People have their strengths, and also their shortcomings.
translations.idiomatic: Every person has qualities in which he excels, and qualities in which he falls short.

translations.literal: What can be deemed entirely fitting?
translations.idiomatic: What in this world can be called entirely fitting?

translations.literal: How can things be made whole?
translations.idiomatic: How can anything be made perfect?'

sentences.zh: 天尚不全，故世為屋，不成三瓦而陳之，以應之天。

translations.literal: Even heaven is not perfect; therefore when people of the world build houses, they do not complete three tiles before setting them out, in order to correspond to heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Even heaven itself is not perfect. When people build houses, they leave the tiles incomplete — a symbolic gesture to correspond with the imperfection of heaven.

sentences.zh: 天下有階，物不全乃生也。」

translations.literal: All under heaven has gradations; things are born precisely because they are not perfect.'
translations.idiomatic: All under heaven is graded in degrees. Things come into being precisely because they are not perfect.'

sentences.zh: 褚先生曰：漁者舉網而得神龜，龜自見夢宋元王，元王召博士衛平告以夢龜狀，平運式，定日月，分衡度，視吉凶，占龜與物色同，平諫王留神龜以為國重寶，美矣。

translations.literal: Master Chu says: The fisherman raised his net and caught a divine tortoise; the tortoise appeared of its own accord in a dream to King Yuan of Song; King Yuan summoned the Erudite Wei Ping and told him of the dream tortoise's appearance; Wei Ping operated his divination board, determined the positions of sun and moon, divided the measurements on the balance, examined the fortune and misfortune, and his divination of the tortoise matched its physical appearance; Wei Ping advised the king to keep the divine tortoise as a great treasure of the state — how admirable!
translations.idiomatic: Master Chu says: The fisherman hauled up his net and found a divine tortoise. The tortoise appeared of its own accord in King Yuan's dream. King Yuan summoned the Erudite Wei Ping and described the tortoise he had seen. Wei Ping worked his divination board, fixed the positions of the sun and moon, calibrated the balance, examined the signs of fortune and misfortune, and his divination matched the tortoise's appearance exactly. He counseled the king to keep the divine tortoise as a great treasure of state. How admirable!

sentences.zh: 古者筮必稱龜者，以其令名，所從來久矣。

translations.literal: In ancient times, divination invariably invoked the tortoise, on account of its august name; this practice has endured for a very long time.
translations.idiomatic: In ancient times, divination always invoked the tortoise, on account of its venerable name — a practice of great antiquity.

translations.literal: I have narrated and compiled this as a treatise.
translations.idiomatic: I have set down this narrative as a treatise.

sentences.zh: 三月二月正月十二月十一月中關內高外下四月首仰足開肣開首俛大五月橫吉首俛大六月七月八月九月十月

translations.literal: Third month, second month, first month, twelfth month, eleventh month: the inner barrier is high and the outer is low. Fourth month: head raised, feet spread, belly open, head bowed — greatly auspicious. Fifth month: horizontal — auspicious; head bowed — greatly auspicious. Sixth month, seventh month, eighth month, ninth month, tenth month.
translations.idiomatic: Third month, second month, first month, twelfth month, eleventh month: the inner barrier is high and the outer low. Fourth month: head raised, feet spread, belly open, head bowed — greatly auspicious. Fifth month: horizontal — auspicious; head bowed — greatly auspicious. Sixth month, seventh month, eighth month, ninth month, tenth month.

sentences.zh: 卜禁曰：子亥戌不可以卜及殺龜。

translations.literal: The divination prohibitions state: On the zi, hai, and xu days, one may not divine or kill a tortoise.
translations.idiomatic: The divination prohibitions state: On zi, hai, and xu days, one must not perform divination or kill a tortoise.

translations.literal: At midday, it is as though the divination has already been consumed.
translations.idiomatic: At midday, divination should be performed as if the meal has ended.

sentences.zh: 暮昏龜之徼也，不可以卜。

translations.literal: At dusk and nightfall is the tortoise's boundary; one may not divine at this time.
translations.idiomatic: Dusk and nightfall mark the tortoise's boundary — divination must not be performed at these times.

translations.literal: On geng and xin days, one may kill the tortoise and drill it.
translations.idiomatic: On geng and xin days, one may kill the tortoise and bore into its shell.

sentences.zh: 常以月旦祓龜，先以清水澡之，以卵祓之，乃持龜而遂之，若常以為祖。

translations.literal: One should always purify the tortoise on the first day of the month: first wash it with clean water, then purify it with an egg, then hold the tortoise and proceed with it, treating it always as an ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: On the first day of each month, one should purify the tortoise. First wash it with clean water, then purify it with an egg, then hold the tortoise and proceed — always treating it with the reverence due an ancestor.

sentences.zh: 人若已卜不中，皆祓之以卵，東向立，灼以荊若剛木，土卵指之者三，持龜以卵周環之，祝曰：「今日吉，謹以粱卵焍黃祓去玉靈之不祥。」

translations.literal: If a person has divined and the result was inaccurate, one should purify the tortoise with an egg: stand facing east, scorch it with thorn or hardwood, point the earth-egg at it three times, hold the tortoise and circle the egg around it, and pray: 'Today is auspicious. Respectfully with grain and egg of deep yellow, I purify and remove the inauspicious from the Jade Spirit.'
translations.idiomatic: If a divination has proven inaccurate, one purifies the tortoise with an egg. Stand facing east, scorch it with thorn or hardwood, point the earthen egg at it three times, hold the tortoise and circle the egg around it, and recite: 'Today is auspicious. With grain and deep-yellow egg, I respectfully purify and banish all ill fortune from the Jade Spirit.'

sentences.zh: 玉靈必信以誠，知萬事之情，辯兆皆可占。

translations.literal: The Jade Spirit must be trusted with sincerity; it knows the circumstances of ten thousand affairs, and all crack-omens it discerns can be divined.
translations.idiomatic: The Jade Spirit must be approached with sincerity and trust. It knows the truth of ten thousand affairs, and every crack-omen it produces can be divined.

sentences.zh: 不信不誠，則燒玉靈，揚其灰，以徵后龜。

translations.literal: If it is untrusted and treated without sincerity, then burn the Jade Spirit, scatter its ashes, and summon a new tortoise.
translations.idiomatic: If it proves untrustworthy and is treated without sincerity, then burn the Jade Spirit, scatter its ashes, and summon a new tortoise.

sentences.zh: 其卜必北向，龜甲必尺二寸。

translations.literal: The divination must be performed facing north, and the tortoise shell must be one chi two cun.
translations.idiomatic: Divination must be performed facing north, and the tortoise shell must measure one chi and two cun.

sentences.zh: 卜先以造灼鉆，鉆中已，又灼龜首，各三；

translations.literal: For divination, first use a tool to scorch and drill; when the drilling is done, then scorch the head of the tortoise, three times each;
translations.idiomatic: To begin the divination, first use the boring tool to scorch and drill. When the drilling is complete, scorch the head of the tortoise shell — three times each;

sentences.zh: 又復灼所鉆中曰正身，灼首曰正足，各三。

translations.literal: then scorch again at the center of the drilled spot, calling it 'aligning the body,' and scorch the head, calling it 'aligning the feet,' three times each.
translations.idiomatic: then scorch again at the center of the drilled spot — this is called 'aligning the body' — and scorch the head — called 'aligning the feet' — three times each.

sentences.zh: 即以造三周龜，祝曰：「假之玉靈夫子。

translations.literal: Then use the tool to circle the tortoise three times and pray: 'I borrow from you, Master Jade Spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Then circle the tortoise three times with the tool and recite the invocation: 'I call upon you, Master Jade Spirit.

sentences.zh: 夫子玉靈，荊灼而心，令而先知。

translations.literal: Master Jade Spirit, the thorn scorches your heart, and you command foreknowledge.
translations.idiomatic: Master Jade Spirit, the thorn scorches your heart and grants you foreknowledge.

sentences.zh: 而上行於天，下行於淵，諸靈數，莫如汝信。

translations.literal: You travel above through the heavens and below through the abyss; among all the spirits in number, none is as trustworthy as you.
translations.idiomatic: You travel above through the heavens and below through the abyss. Among all spirits, none is as trustworthy as you.

translations.literal: Today is an auspicious day; I perform one auspicious divination.
translations.idiomatic: Today is an auspicious day. I now perform a propitious divination.

sentences.zh: 某欲卜某，即得而喜，不得而悔。

translations.literal: So-and-so wishes to divine about such-and-such; if obtained, there is joy; if not obtained, there is regret.
translations.idiomatic: So-and-so wishes to divine about such-and-such a matter. If the answer is favorable, there will be joy; if unfavorable, regret.

sentences.zh: 即得，發鄉我身長大，首足收人皆上偶。

translations.literal: If obtained, let the cracks open toward my body, long and large, with head and feet gathered — all above matching in pairs.
translations.idiomatic: If favorable, let the cracks open toward me, long and wide, with head and feet gathered — all matching symmetrically above.

sentences.zh: 不得，發鄉我身挫折，中外不相應，首足滅去。」

translations.literal: If not obtained, let the cracks toward my body be broken and bent, inside and outside not matching, head and feet vanishing.'
translations.idiomatic: If unfavorable, let the cracks toward me be broken and bent, inner and outer not matching, head and feet vanishing.'

sentences.zh: 靈龜卜祝曰：「假之靈龜，五巫五靈，不如神龜之靈，知人死，知人生。

translations.literal: The divine tortoise divination invocation says: 'I borrow from the divine tortoise. The five shamans and five spirits are not as divine as the spirit of the tortoise, which knows human death and knows human life.
translations.idiomatic: The invocation for the divine tortoise divination runs: 'I call upon the divine tortoise. The five shamans and five spirits cannot match the divine power of the tortoise, which knows the death of men and knows their life.

translations.literal: So-and-so's person is of good augury; so-and-so wishes to seek such-and-such a thing.
translations.idiomatic: So-and-so's augury is propitious. So-and-so seeks such-and-such.

sentences.zh: 即得也，頭見足發，內外相應；

translations.literal: If obtained, the head is visible and the feet extend, inside and outside corresponding;
translations.idiomatic: If favorable, the head appears and the feet extend, inner and outer matching;

sentences.zh: 即不得也，頭仰足肣，內外自垂。

translations.literal: if not obtained, the head is raised and the feet are drawn in, inside and outside drooping down.
translations.idiomatic: if unfavorable, the head is raised and the feet are drawn in, inner and outer drooping downward.

translations.literal: This can be divined.'
translations.idiomatic: This may be divined.'

sentences.zh: 卜占病者祝曰：「今某病困。

translations.literal: The invocation for divining on behalf of a sick person says: 'Now so-and-so is gravely ill.
translations.idiomatic: The invocation for divining on behalf of a sick person runs: 'So-and-so is now gravely ill.

sentences.zh: 死，首上開，內外交駭，身節折；

translations.literal: If the person will die, the head opens upward, inside and outside are alarmed in confusion, and the body joints are broken;
translations.idiomatic: If death is indicated, the head of the crack opens upward, inner and outer lines cross in alarm, and the body joints are broken;

translations.literal: if the person will not die, the head is raised and the feet are drawn in.'
translations.idiomatic: if the patient will survive, the head is raised and the feet are drawn in.'

sentences.zh: 卜病者祟曰：「今病有祟無呈，無祟有呈。

translations.literal: The invocation for divining a patient's curse says: 'The current illness either has a curse but no outward sign, or has no curse but does have an outward sign.
translations.idiomatic: The invocation for divining whether a patient's illness is caused by a curse runs: 'This illness may have a curse without visible signs, or it may show signs without any curse.

sentences.zh: 兆有中祟有內，外祟有外。」

translations.literal: If the omen has a center, the curse is internal; if the curse is external, the omen shows it on the outside.'
translations.idiomatic: If the omen has markings at the center, the curse is internal. If the curse is external, the omen shows it on the outer portion.'

translations.literal: Divining whether a prisoner will be released or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether a prisoner will be released.

translations.literal: If the prisoner will not be released: horizontal, auspicious, settled;
translations.idiomatic: If the prisoner will not be released, the crack is horizontal — auspicious and settled;

translations.literal: if the prisoner will be released: feet spread, head raised, with external markings.
translations.idiomatic: if the prisoner will be released, the feet spread, the head is raised, and there are external markings.

translations.literal: Divining whether one will obtain sought-after wealth and goods, and what one is due to receive.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether sought-after wealth and goods will be obtained.

translations.literal: If obtained: head raised, feet spread, inside and outside corresponding;
translations.idiomatic: If the goods will be obtained, the head is raised and the feet spread, inner and outer matching;

translations.literal: if not obtained, the manifest omen has the head raised and feet drawn in.
translations.idiomatic: if not obtained, the omen shows the head raised and the feet drawn in.

translations.literal: Divining whether one will sell or buy servants, concubines, horses, or oxen.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on selling or buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen.

sentences.zh: 得之，首仰足開，內外相應；

translations.literal: If successful: head raised, feet spread, inside and outside corresponding;
translations.idiomatic: If the transaction will succeed, the head is raised and the feet spread, inner and outer matching;

sentences.zh: 不得，首仰足肣，呈兆若橫吉安。

translations.literal: if unsuccessful, the head is raised and feet drawn in; the manifest omen may be horizontal, auspicious, and settled.
translations.idiomatic: if unsuccessful, the head is raised and the feet are drawn in. The manifest omen may be horizontal — auspicious and settled.

sentences.zh: 卜擊盜聚若干人，在某所，今某將卒若干人，往擊之。

translations.literal: Divining about attacking a band of thieves, a certain number of people, at a certain place; now a certain commander leads a certain number of soldiers to go and attack them.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on attacking a band of brigands — a certain number gathered at a certain place. A certain commander now leads a certain number of soldiers to go and strike them.

sentences.zh: 當勝，首仰足開身正，內自橋，外下；

translations.literal: If victory is indicated: head raised, feet spread, body upright, the inside arches up, the outside goes down;
translations.idiomatic: If victory is indicated, the head is raised, the feet spread, and the body is upright — the inner line arches up while the outer descends;

sentences.zh: 不勝，足肣首仰，身首內下外高。

translations.literal: if defeat is indicated: feet drawn in, head raised, the body and head are low inside and high outside.
translations.idiomatic: if defeat is indicated, the feet are drawn in, the head raised, and the body and head show the inner line low and the outer high.

translations.literal: Divining whether one should travel or not travel.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether one should set out on a journey or not.

translations.literal: If one should travel: head and feet spread;
translations.idiomatic: If travel is indicated, the head and feet are spread;

sentences.zh: 不行，足肣首仰，若橫吉安，安不行。

translations.literal: if one should not travel: feet drawn in, head raised; if horizontal, auspicious, and settled — settled means do not travel.
translations.idiomatic: if one should not travel, the feet are drawn in and the head raised. If the crack is horizontal, auspicious, and settled — 'settled' means do not travel.

translations.literal: Divining whether one will encounter the bandits when going to attack them, or not encounter them.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether one will encounter the brigands when setting out to attack them.

translations.literal: If one will encounter them: head raised, feet drawn in, with external markings;
translations.idiomatic: If the brigands will be encountered, the head is raised, the feet drawn in, with external markings;

translations.literal: if one will not encounter them: feet spread, head raised.
translations.idiomatic: if they will not be encountered, the feet spread and the head is raised.

translations.literal: Divining whether one will spot the bandits when going to scout them, or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether one will spot the brigands when going to scout for them.

translations.literal: If one will spot them: head raised, feet drawn in, the drawn-in part prevails with external markings;
translations.idiomatic: If they will be spotted, the head is raised, the feet drawn in — the drawn-in part prevails, with external markings;

translations.literal: if one will not spot them: feet spread, head raised.
translations.idiomatic: if they will not be spotted, the feet spread and the head is raised.

translations.literal: Divining whether the reported bandits will come or not come.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether reported brigands will arrive or not.

translations.literal: If they will come: outer high, inner low, feet drawn in, head raised;
translations.idiomatic: If they will come, the outer line is high and the inner low, the feet drawn in, and the head raised;

sentences.zh: 不來，足開首仰，若橫吉安，期之自次。

translations.literal: if they will not come: feet spread, head raised; if horizontal, auspicious, and settled — expect them to settle in their own place.
translations.idiomatic: if they will not come, the feet spread and the head is raised. If horizontal, auspicious, and settled — expect them to remain where they are.

translations.literal: Divining whether one will transfer and leave office, or not leave.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether one will be transferred and leave office or remain.

translations.literal: If one will leave: feet spread, with drawn-in parts outside, head raised;
translations.idiomatic: If departure is indicated, the feet spread with drawn-in parts on the outer side, and the head is raised;

sentences.zh: 不去，自去，即足肣，呈兆若橫吉安。

translations.literal: if one will not leave or will leave of one's own accord: feet drawn in; the manifest omen is horizontal, auspicious, and settled.
translations.idiomatic: if one will not leave, or will depart voluntarily, the feet are drawn in; the manifest omen is horizontal — auspicious and settled.

translations.literal: Divining whether remaining in office will still be auspicious or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether remaining in office will prove auspicious.

translations.literal: If auspicious: the manifest omen has the body upright, or is horizontal, auspicious, and settled;
translations.idiomatic: If auspicious, the manifest omen shows the body upright, or is horizontal — auspicious and settled;

translations.literal: if inauspicious: the body joints are broken, head raised, feet spread.
translations.idiomatic: if inauspicious, the body joints are broken, the head raised, and the feet spread.

translations.literal: Divining whether dwelling in one's house and home will be auspicious or inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether dwelling in one's house and home will be auspicious.

translations.literal: If auspicious: the manifest omen has the body upright, or is horizontal, auspicious, and settled;
translations.idiomatic: If auspicious, the manifest omen shows the body upright, or is horizontal — auspicious and settled;

translations.literal: if inauspicious: the body joints are broken, head raised, feet spread.
translations.idiomatic: if inauspicious, the body joints are broken, the head raised, and the feet spread.

translations.literal: Divining whether the grain crops will ripen during the year or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether the grain crops will ripen during the year.

sentences.zh: 孰，首仰足開，內外自橋外自垂；

translations.literal: If they will ripen: head raised, feet spread, inside arches up and outside droops down;
translations.idiomatic: If the crops will ripen, the head is raised and feet spread — the inner line arches up while the outer droops;

translations.literal: if they will not ripen: feet drawn in, head raised, with external markings.
translations.idiomatic: if they will not ripen, the feet are drawn in, the head raised, with external markings.

translations.literal: Divining whether there will be an epidemic among the people during the year or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether an epidemic will strike the people during the year.

sentences.zh: 疫，首仰足肣，身節有彊外；

translations.literal: If there will be an epidemic: head raised, feet drawn in, body joints show stiffness on the outside;
translations.idiomatic: If an epidemic is indicated, the head is raised, the feet drawn in, and the body joints show rigidity on the outer portion;

translations.literal: if there will be no epidemic: body upright, head raised, feet spread.
translations.idiomatic: if no epidemic is indicated, the body is upright, the head raised, and the feet spread.

translations.literal: Divining whether there will be war during the year or no war.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether there will be war during the year.

translations.literal: If there will be no war: the manifest omen is horizontal, auspicious, and settled;
translations.idiomatic: If no war is indicated, the manifest omen is horizontal — auspicious and settled;

sentences.zh: 有兵，首仰足開，身作外彊情。

translations.literal: If there will be war: head raised, feet spread, the body shows a rigid external aspect.
translations.idiomatic: If war is indicated, the head is raised, the feet spread, and the body shows a rigid external aspect.

translations.literal: Divining whether meeting a person of high rank will be auspicious or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether meeting a person of high rank will prove auspicious.

sentences.zh: 吉，足開首仰，身正，內自橋；

translations.literal: If auspicious: feet spread, head raised, body upright, inside arches up;
translations.idiomatic: If auspicious, the feet spread, the head is raised, the body upright, and the inner line arches up;

sentences.zh: 不吉，首仰，身節折，足肣有外，若無漁。

translations.literal: if inauspicious: head raised, body joints broken, feet drawn in with external markings, or there is no catch.
translations.idiomatic: if inauspicious, the head is raised, the body joints broken, the feet drawn in with external markings, or there is no yield.

translations.literal: Divining whether a petition to a person will be granted or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether a petition to another person will be granted.

translations.literal: If granted: head raised, feet spread, inside arches up;
translations.idiomatic: If the petition will be granted, the head is raised, the feet spread, and the inner line arches up;

translations.literal: if not granted: head raised, feet drawn in, with external markings.
translations.idiomatic: if not granted, the head is raised, the feet drawn in, with external markings.

translations.literal: Divining whether a fugitive being pursued will be caught or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether a fugitive will be caught.

translations.literal: If the fugitive will be caught: head raised, feet drawn in, inside and outside corresponding;
translations.idiomatic: If the fugitive will be caught, the head is raised, the feet drawn in, inner and outer matching;

sentences.zh: 不得，首仰足開，若橫吉安。

translations.literal: if not caught: head raised, feet spread, or horizontal, auspicious, and settled.
translations.idiomatic: if not caught, the head is raised and the feet spread, or the crack is horizontal — auspicious and settled.

translations.literal: Divining whether fishing or hunting will yield a catch or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether fishing or hunting will yield a catch.

translations.literal: If a catch will be made: head raised, feet spread, inside and outside corresponding;
translations.idiomatic: If a catch will be made, the head is raised and the feet spread, inner and outer matching;

sentences.zh: 不得，足肣首仰，若橫吉安。

translations.literal: if no catch: feet drawn in, head raised, or horizontal, auspicious, and settled.
translations.idiomatic: if no catch, the feet are drawn in and the head raised, or the crack is horizontal — auspicious and settled.

translations.literal: Divining whether one will encounter bandits on a journey or not.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether one will encounter brigands while traveling.

sentences.zh: 遇，首仰足開，身節折，外高內下；

translations.literal: If one will encounter them: head raised, feet spread, body joints broken, outer high and inner low;
translations.idiomatic: If brigands will be encountered, the head is raised, the feet spread, the body joints broken, the outer portion high and the inner low;

translations.literal: If one will not encounter them: the manifest omen appears.
translations.idiomatic: If no brigands will be encountered, the manifest omen appears as normal.

translations.literal: Divining whether it will rain or not rain.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether it will rain.

translations.literal: If it will rain: head raised with external markings, outer high and inner low;
translations.idiomatic: If rain is indicated, the head is raised with external markings — the outer portion high and the inner low;

sentences.zh: 不雨，首仰足開，若橫吉安。

translations.literal: if it will not rain: head raised, feet spread, or horizontal, auspicious, and settled.
translations.idiomatic: if no rain, the head is raised and the feet spread, or the crack is horizontal — auspicious and settled.

translations.literal: Divining whether the rain will clear or not clear.
translations.idiomatic: Divination on whether the rain will clear.

translations.literal: If the rain will clear: the manifest omen has feet spread and head raised;
translations.idiomatic: If the rain will clear, the manifest omen shows the feet spread and head raised;

translations.literal: if it will not clear: horizontal, auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: if the rain will not clear, the crack is horizontal — auspicious.

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'horizontal, auspicious, and settled.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'horizontal — auspicious and settled.'

sentences.zh: 以占病，病甚者一日不死；

translations.literal: Using it to divine illness: one who is seriously ill will not die within one day;
translations.idiomatic: When used to divine illness, a seriously ill patient will not die within one day;

translations.literal: one whose illness is not serious will recover on the divined day and will not die.
translations.idiomatic: one whose illness is not serious will recover on the divined day and will not die.

sentences.zh: 系者重罪不出，輕罪環出；

translations.literal: For prisoners: those with serious offenses will not be released, those with minor offenses will soon be released;
translations.idiomatic: For prisoners, those with serious offenses will not be released, while those with minor offenses will soon go free;

translations.literal: if after one day no release, then prolonged detention but no harm will come.
translations.idiomatic: if after one day there is still no release, the detention will be prolonged but no harm will come.

sentences.zh: 求財物買臣妾馬牛，一日環得；

translations.literal: Seeking wealth or buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen: within one day one will obtain them;
translations.idiomatic: When seeking wealth or buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen, the result will be obtained within one day;

translations.literal: after one day they will not be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: beyond one day, the opportunity will be lost.

translations.literal: Those planning to travel will not travel.
translations.idiomatic: Those who planned to travel will not set out.

translations.literal: Those expected to arrive will come soon;
translations.idiomatic: Those expected to arrive will come shortly;

translations.literal: if they have not arrived by mealtime, they will not come.
translations.idiomatic: if they have not arrived by mealtime, they will not come.

translations.literal: Attacking bandits: do not go; if one goes, one will not encounter them;
translations.idiomatic: As for attacking brigands: do not go. If one goes, the brigands will not be encountered;

translations.literal: reported bandits will not come.
translations.idiomatic: reported brigands will not come.

translations.literal: Official transfer: no transfer will occur.
translations.idiomatic: As for an official transfer: there will be no transfer.

translations.literal: Remaining in office and dwelling at home are both auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Remaining in office and dwelling at home are both auspicious.

translations.literal: The year's grain crops will not ripen.
translations.idiomatic: The year's grain crops will not ripen.

translations.literal: Regarding epidemic illness among the people: there will be no illness.
translations.idiomatic: As for epidemic illness among the people: there will be none.

translations.literal: There will be no war during the year.
translations.idiomatic: There will be no war during the year.

translations.literal: Meeting a person of rank and traveling: do not go — there will be no joy.
translations.idiomatic: As for meeting a person of rank: do not go — no joy will come of it.

translations.literal: Petitioning a person: do not go — the petition will not be granted.
translations.idiomatic: As for petitioning another: do not go — it will not be granted.

translations.literal: Pursuing a fugitive, fishing, or hunting: nothing will be caught.
translations.idiomatic: Pursuing a fugitive, fishing, or hunting will yield nothing.

translations.literal: Traveling: one will not encounter bandits.
translations.idiomatic: While traveling, one will not encounter brigands.

translations.literal: Regarding rain: it will not rain.
translations.idiomatic: As for rain: it will not rain.

translations.literal: Regarding clearing: it will not clear.
translations.idiomatic: As for clearing: the rain will not clear.

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'manifest omen.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'manifest omen.'

translations.literal: The sick person will not die.
translations.idiomatic: The patient will not die.

translations.literal: The prisoner will be released.
translations.idiomatic: The prisoner will go free.

translations.literal: The traveler will travel.
translations.idiomatic: The traveler will set out on the journey.

translations.literal: The expected person will come.
translations.idiomatic: The awaited person will arrive.

translations.literal: Market purchases will be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Purchases at the market will succeed.

translations.literal: A pursued fugitive will be caught; after one day, the fugitive will not be caught.
translations.idiomatic: A pursued fugitive will be caught, but if not found within one day, the search will fail.

translations.literal: Inquiring about a traveler: the traveler has not arrived.
translations.idiomatic: As for inquiring about a traveler: the traveler has not yet arrived.

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'pillar piercing through.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'pillar piercing through.'

translations.literal: Divining illness: the patient will not die.
translations.idiomatic: When divining illness, the patient will not die.

translations.literal: The prisoner will be released.
translations.idiomatic: The prisoner will go free.

translations.literal: The traveler will travel.
translations.idiomatic: The traveler will set out on the journey.

translations.literal: The expected person will come.
translations.idiomatic: The awaited person will arrive.

translations.literal: Market purchases will not be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Purchases at the market will not succeed.

translations.literal: Those who are worried need not worry.
translations.idiomatic: Those who are worried need not be anxious.

translations.literal: A pursued fugitive will not be caught.
translations.idiomatic: The pursuit of a fugitive will not succeed.

sentences.zh: 命曰首仰足肣有內無外。

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'head raised, feet drawn in, with internal markings and no external.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'head raised, feet drawn in — internal markings present, external absent.'

translations.literal: Divining illness: even if seriously ill, the patient will not die.
translations.idiomatic: When divining illness, even a seriously ill patient will not die.

translations.literal: The prisoner will be released.
translations.idiomatic: The prisoner's bonds will be loosened.

sentences.zh: 求財物買臣妾馬牛不得。

translations.literal: Seeking wealth or buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen: they will not be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Seeking wealth or buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen will not succeed.

translations.literal: The traveler will hear a report and not travel.
translations.idiomatic: The traveler will receive word and decide not to set out.

translations.literal: The expected person will not come.
translations.idiomatic: The awaited person will not arrive.

translations.literal: Reported bandits will not come.
translations.idiomatic: Reported brigands will not come.

translations.literal: Upon hearing the report, they will not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Despite the reports, they will not arrive.

translations.literal: Regarding transfer of office: upon hearing the report, there will be no transfer.
translations.idiomatic: As for an official transfer: despite the report, no transfer will take place.

translations.literal: Remaining in office: there will be worries.
translations.idiomatic: Remaining in office will bring worries.

translations.literal: Dwelling at home: there will be many disasters.
translations.idiomatic: Dwelling at home will bring many calamities.

translations.literal: The year's grain crops will ripen on average.
translations.idiomatic: The year's grain harvest will be moderate.

translations.literal: The people's epidemic illness: there will be much sickness.
translations.idiomatic: There will be much sickness from epidemic illness among the people.

translations.literal: There will be war during the year; upon hearing the reports, it will not be resolved.
translations.idiomatic: There will be war during the year, and despite reports, hostilities will not be resolved.

translations.literal: Meeting a person of rank: auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Meeting a person of rank will be auspicious.

translations.literal: Petitioning: do not go; if one goes, one will not receive favorable words.
translations.idiomatic: As for petitioning: do not go. If one does go, no favorable words will be received.

translations.literal: Pursuing a fugitive: the fugitive will not be caught.
translations.idiomatic: The pursuit of a fugitive will not succeed.

translations.literal: Fishing or hunting: nothing will be caught.
translations.idiomatic: Fishing or hunting will yield nothing.

translations.literal: Traveling: one will not encounter bandits.
translations.idiomatic: While traveling, one will not encounter brigands.

translations.literal: Regarding rain: it will not rain heavily.
translations.idiomatic: As for rain: it will not rain heavily.

translations.literal: Regarding clearing: it will not clear.
translations.idiomatic: As for clearing: the rain will not clear.

translations.literal: Therefore the character 'mo' in all cases is written as 'head prepared.'
translations.idiomatic: Therefore in all these entries, the character for 'evening' is consistently written as 'head prepared.'

sentences.zh: 問之曰，備者仰也，故定以為仰。

translations.literal: When asked about this, the answer is: 'prepared' means 'raised,' and so it is fixed as 'raised.'
translations.idiomatic: When asked, the explanation is: 'prepared' means 'raised,' and so it has been established as 'raised.'

translations.literal: This is a private record.
translations.idiomatic: This is a private notation.

sentences.zh: 命曰首仰足肣有內無外。

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'head raised, feet drawn in, with internal markings and no external.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'head raised, feet drawn in — internal markings present, external absent.'

translations.literal: Divining illness: even if seriously ill, the patient will not die.
translations.idiomatic: When divining illness, even a seriously ill patient will not die.

translations.literal: The prisoner will not be released.
translations.idiomatic: The prisoner will not be released.

translations.literal: Seeking wealth or buying servants and concubines: they will not be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Seeking wealth or buying servants and concubines will not succeed.

translations.literal: The traveler will not travel.
translations.idiomatic: The traveler will not set out.

translations.literal: The expected person will not come.
translations.idiomatic: The awaited person will not arrive.

translations.literal: Attacking bandits: they will not be seen.
translations.idiomatic: When attacking brigands, they will not be found.

translations.literal: Reported bandits are said to be coming; within there is alarm, but they will not come.
translations.idiomatic: Reports say brigands are coming — there is alarm within, but they will not come.

translations.literal: Official transfer: there will be no transfer.
translations.idiomatic: As for an official transfer: there will be none.

translations.literal: Remaining in office and dwelling at home: auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Remaining in office and dwelling at home will be auspicious.

translations.literal: The year's grain crops will not ripen.
translations.idiomatic: The year's grain crops will not ripen.

translations.literal: Regarding epidemic illness among the people: there will be much severe sickness.
translations.idiomatic: There will be severe epidemic illness among the people.

translations.literal: There will be no war during the year.

translations.literal: Slightly auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: This is slightly auspicious.

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'carrying position.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'carrying position.'

translations.literal: Using it to divine illness: there will soon be recovery and no death.
translations.idiomatic: When used to divine illness, the patient will soon recover and will not die.

translations.literal: The prisoner will be released.
translations.idiomatic: The prisoner will go free.

sentences.zh: 求財物買臣妾馬牛請謁追亡人漁獵得。

translations.literal: Seeking wealth, buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen, petitioning, pursuing a fugitive, fishing, or hunting: all will succeed.
translations.idiomatic: Seeking wealth, buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen, petitioning, pursuing a fugitive, fishing, and hunting will all succeed.

translations.literal: The traveler will travel.
translations.idiomatic: The traveler will set out on the journey.

translations.literal: The expected person will come.
translations.idiomatic: The awaited person will arrive.

translations.literal: Attacking bandits: the forces will meet but not engage.
translations.idiomatic: When attacking brigands, the forces will encounter each other but will not engage in battle.

translations.literal: Reported bandits: they will come.
translations.idiomatic: Reported brigands will indeed come.

translations.literal: Official transfer: there will be a transfer.
translations.idiomatic: An official transfer will take place.

translations.literal: Dwelling at home: there will be worries.
translations.idiomatic: Dwelling at home will bring worries.

translations.literal: Meeting a person of rank: auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Meeting a person of rank will be auspicious.

translations.literal: The year's crops will ripen.
translations.idiomatic: The year's grain crops will ripen.

translations.literal: The people will have no epidemic illness.
translations.idiomatic: There will be no epidemic illness among the people.

translations.literal: There will be no war during the year.
translations.idiomatic: There will be no war during the year.

translations.literal: Traveling: one will not encounter bandits.
translations.idiomatic: While traveling, one will not encounter brigands.

translations.literal: Regarding rain: it will not rain.
translations.idiomatic: As for rain: it will not rain.

translations.literal: Regarding clearing: the rain will clear.
translations.idiomatic: As for clearing: the skies will clear.

translations.literal: Auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: This is auspicious.

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'root obstruction.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'root obstruction.'

translations.literal: Using it to divine a sick person: the patient will not die.
translations.idiomatic: When used to divine illness, the patient will not die.

translations.literal: The prisoner will be held for a long time, but will not be harmed.
translations.idiomatic: The prisoner will be detained for a long time, but will come to no harm.

sentences.zh: 求財物買臣妾馬牛請謁追亡人漁獵不得。

translations.literal: Seeking wealth, buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen, petitioning, pursuing a fugitive, fishing, or hunting: none will succeed.
translations.idiomatic: Seeking wealth, buying servants, concubines, horses, or oxen, petitioning, pursuing a fugitive, fishing, and hunting will all fail.

translations.literal: The traveler will not travel.
translations.idiomatic: The traveler will not set out.

translations.literal: The expected person will not come.
translations.idiomatic: The awaited person will not arrive.

translations.literal: Attacking bandits: the bandits move and the forces do not engage.
translations.idiomatic: When attacking brigands, the brigands will move away and the forces will not engage.

translations.literal: Reported bandits: they will not come.
translations.idiomatic: Reported brigands will not come.

translations.literal: Official transfer: there will be no transfer.
translations.idiomatic: As for an official transfer: there will be none.

translations.literal: Dwelling at home: auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Dwelling at home will be auspicious.

translations.literal: The year's grain harvest will be moderate.
translations.idiomatic: The year's grain harvest will be average.

translations.literal: Epidemic illness among the people: there will be no deaths.
translations.idiomatic: There will be epidemic illness among the people, but no deaths.

translations.literal: Meeting a person of rank: the meeting will not take place.
translations.idiomatic: One will seek to meet a person of rank but will not succeed.

translations.literal: Traveling: one will not encounter bandits.
translations.idiomatic: While traveling, one will not encounter brigands.

translations.literal: Regarding rain: it will not rain.
translations.idiomatic: As for rain: it will not rain.

translations.literal: Inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: This is inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 命曰首仰足肣外高內下。

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'head raised, feet drawn in, outer high and inner low.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'head raised, feet drawn in — outer portion high, inner portion low.'

translations.literal: Divining: there will be worries, but no harm.
translations.idiomatic: There will be worries, but no real harm.

translations.literal: The traveler will not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: The traveler will not arrive.

translations.literal: If the illness is prolonged, the patient will die.
translations.idiomatic: If the illness drags on, the patient will die.

translations.literal: Seeking wealth or goods: they will not be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Seeking wealth or goods will not succeed.

translations.literal: Meeting a person of rank: auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Meeting a person of rank will be auspicious.

translations.literal: This configuration is called 'outer high, inner low.'
translations.idiomatic: This crack pattern is termed 'outer high, inner low.'

translations.literal: Divining illness: the patient will not die; there is a curse.
translations.idiomatic: When divining illness, the patient will not die, but there is a curse at work.

sentences.zh: 命曰頭見足發有內外相應。
求財物買臣妾馬牛請謁追亡人漁獵不得。
命曰呈兆首仰足開外高內下。
求財物買臣妾馬牛，相見不會。
徙官居官家室見貴人不吉。
命曰首仰足肣身折內外相應。
求財物買臣妾馬牛漁獵不得。
命曰橫吉內外相應自橋榆仰上柱足肣。
求財物買臣妾馬牛請謁追亡人漁獵不得。
命曰頭仰足肣內外自垂。
命曰內高外下疾輕足發。
命曰內自舉外來正足發。
此橫吉上柱外內自舉足肣。
此橫吉上柱外內自舉柱足以作。
此橫吉上柱載正身節折內外自舉。
以卜病者，卜日不死，其一日乃死。
此橫吉上柱足肣內自舉外自垂。
以卜病者，卜日不死，其一日乃死。
大論曰：外者人也，內者自我也；
大法，病者，足肣者生，足開者死。
行者，足開至，足肣者不至。
有求，足開得，足肣者不得。
其卜病也，足開而死者，內高而外下也。
